benchmarks.name: simple
benchmarks.description: Return-only baseline
benchmarks.hypothesis: Captures the staged harness floor for baseline subtraction

benchmarks.name: memory_pair_sum
benchmarks.description: Fixed-size two-word memory baseline
benchmarks.hypothesis: Minimal fixed-width load pair with almost no control-flow

benchmarks.name: bitcount
benchmarks.description: Kernighan popcount over generated u64 input
benchmarks.hypothesis: Favors stronger scalar optimization and loop code generation

benchmarks.name: log2_fold
benchmarks.description: Fold log2 over generated u64 input
benchmarks.hypothesis: Mixes compares and bit tests with modest control-flow

benchmarks.name: dep_chain_short
benchmarks.description: Short serialized dependency chain over generated u64 input
benchmarks.hypothesis: Long scalar dependencies reduce ILP and stress single-chain scheduling

benchmarks.name: dep_chain_long
benchmarks.description: Long serialized dependency chain over generated u64 input
benchmarks.hypothesis: Scaling dependency depth should amplify backend scheduling differences

benchmarks.name: binary_search
benchmarks.description: Repeated search over sorted data
benchmarks.hypothesis: Branch-heavy search should reward stronger branch-oriented code generation

benchmarks.name: branch_layout
benchmarks.description: Hot/cold branch skew for layout experiments
benchmarks.hypothesis: Skewed branches should expose layout and prediction quality differences

benchmarks.name: switch_dispatch
benchmarks.description: Dense switch dispatch over generated 6-bit values
benchmarks.hypothesis: Dense dispatch should reflect jump-table or compare-ladder lowering quality

benchmarks.name: checksum
benchmarks.description: Internet checksum style reduction
benchmarks.hypothesis: Sequential 16-bit reductions should emphasize scalar memory throughput

benchmarks.name: packet_parse
benchmarks.description: Packet header parsing with bounds checks
benchmarks.hypothesis: Structured parsing with field extraction may favor kernel's packet-oriented JIT path

benchmarks.name: bounds_ladder
benchmarks.description: Record parsing with dense layered bounds checks
benchmarks.hypothesis: Dense bounds checks should stress compare-heavy code generation more than raw memory bandwidth

benchmarks.name: stride_load_4
benchmarks.description: Sequential-stride load pattern with stride 4 over a ring buffer
benchmarks.hypothesis: Mild stride should stay cache-friendly while exposing address-generation cost

benchmarks.name: stride_load_16
benchmarks.description: Wider-stride load pattern with stride 16 over a ring buffer
benchmarks.hypothesis: Wider stride should amplify addressing and prefetch sensitivity

benchmarks.name: spill_pressure
benchmarks.description: Multi-accumulator arithmetic intended to stress register allocation
benchmarks.hypothesis: Register pressure should expose spill/fill quality and register allocator limits

benchmarks.name: multi_acc_4
benchmarks.description: Four-lane accumulator kernel with cross-lane mixing
benchmarks.hypothesis: More independent accumulators should reward ILP-friendly code generation

benchmarks.name: multi_acc_8
benchmarks.description: Eight-lane accumulator kernel with cross-lane mixing
benchmarks.hypothesis: Higher lane count should trade ILP gains against register pressure

benchmarks.name: fibonacci_iter
benchmarks.description: Loop-heavy Fibonacci-style recurrence with bounded iteration count
benchmarks.hypothesis: Scalar recurrence loops should narrow the gap by limiting exploitable ILP

benchmarks.name: fixed_loop_small
benchmarks.description: Short fixed-trip-count recurrence loop
benchmarks.hypothesis: Short fixed loops should keep loop overhead visible relative to the loop body

benchmarks.name: fixed_loop_large
benchmarks.description: Long fixed-trip-count recurrence loop
benchmarks.hypothesis: Larger trip counts should amortize setup overhead and highlight steady-state loop code quality

benchmarks.name: code_clone_2
benchmarks.description: Two-clone repeated scalar block inside the steady-state loop
benchmarks.hypothesis: Modest cloned straight-line code should slightly favor backends with stronger instruction scheduling

benchmarks.name: code_clone_8
benchmarks.description: Eight-clone repeated scalar block inside the steady-state loop
benchmarks.hypothesis: Larger cloned bodies should magnify code-size and straight-line scheduling differences
